findings.description: Password policy weaknesses detected: Minimum password length is 0 (should be ≥12).
findings.recommendation: Configure via Group Policy: Computer Configuration → Windows Settings → Security Settings → Account Policies → Password Policy. Set min length ≥12, max age ≤90 days, enable complexity, set lockout threshold ≤5.
findings.raw_output:
Force user logoff how long after time expires?:       Never
Minimum password age (days):                          0
Maximum password age (days):                          Unlimited
Minimum password length:                              0
Length of password history maintained:                None
Lockout threshold:                                    10
Lockout duration (minutes):                           10
Lockout observation window (minutes):                 10
Computer role:                                        WORKSTATION
The command completed successfully.

findings.description: No obvious EOL software detected from the installed packages list.
findings.recommendation: Maintain an up-to-date software inventory and regularly review EOL dates.

findings.description: Event log configuration issues: Security log is only 20 MB (min recommended: 64 MB); System log is only 20 MB (min recommended: 64 MB); Application log is only 20 MB (min recommended: 64 MB).
findings.recommendation: Configure via GPO: Computer Configuration → Windows Settings → Security Settings → Event Log. Set Security log to ≥128 MB with RetentionDays=0 (overwrite as needed).

findings.description: LSASS is vulnerable to credential dumping: LSASS Protected Process Light (RunAsPPL) is not enabled; Credential Guard is not enabled. Tools like Mimikatz can extract plaintext credentials.
findings.recommendation: Enable RunAsPPL: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa' -Name RunAsPPL -Value 1. Enable Credential Guard via GPO or Device Guard.

findings.description: Administrator group has 2 member(s): DESKTOP-UCPT1TK\Administrator, DESKTOP-UCPT1TK\PC.
findings.recommendation: Periodically review and audit administrator group membership.

findings.description: Name-poisoning attack surface is EXPOSED: LLMNR is enabled; NetBIOS over TCP/IP is enabled on one or more adapters.
findings.recommendation: Disable LLMNR via GPO: Computer Configuration → Administrative Templates → Network → DNS Client → Turn Off Multicast Name Resolution = Enabled. Disable NetBIOS via NIC settings or DHCP option 001.

findings.description: Could not query Windows Update Agent. Manual verification required.
findings.recommendation: Ensure Windows Update service is running and check for pending updates.

findings.description: SMBv1 is disabled. The host is protected against EternalBlue-class attacks.
No SMB shares with overly permissive Everyone/Authenticated Users access.
findings.recommendation: Periodically audit share permissions with Get-SmbShareAccess.

findings.description: Windows Defender is active and signatures are current.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Ensure signatures update automatically.

findings.description: Deprecated protocols enabled: SSL 2.0, SSL 3.0, TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1. These are vulnerable to POODLE, BEAST, DROWN, and downgrade attacks.
findings.recommendation: Disable deprecated protocols via SCHANNEL registry keys. Use IIS Crypto or: New-Item -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.0\Server' -Force | New-ItemProperty -Name Enabled -Value 0 -PropertyType DWORD -Force
findings.raw_output:
SSL 2.0: NOT_SET
SSL 3.0: NOT_SET
TLS 1.0: NOT_SET
TLS 1.1: NOT_SET

findings.description: Privileged group memberships appear within expected limits.
findings.raw_output:
Backup Operators: _EMPTY_
Remote Desktop Users: _EMPTY_
Remote Management Users: _EMPTY_
Power Users: _EMPTY_

findings.description: Weak cipher suites detected: RC4, DES, 3DES, NULL, EXPORT. These enable downgrade attacks and break-in via weak encryption.
findings.recommendation: Disable weak ciphers via SCHANNEL registry. Use IIS Crypto tool or configure via: New-Item -Path 'HKLM:\...\Ciphers\RC4 128/128' -Force | New-ItemProperty -Name Enabled -Value 0 -PropertyType DWORD
findings.raw_output:
RC4: NOT_SET
DES: NOT_SET
3DES: NOT_SET
NULL: NOT_SET
EXPORT: NOT_SET

findings.description: Windows Firewall is enabled on all network profiles (Domain, Private, Public).
findings.recommendation: No action required. Periodically review firewall rules.
